channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok bar fire
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

item.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Oil Prices Rise Amid New U.S.-Iran Attacks; Nvidia, Micron, Sandisk Near Buy Points
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Why Tantita’s pipeline surveillance excites stakeholders
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By Steven Okon TANTITA Security Services Nigeria Ltd’s (TSSNL’s) operations in safeguarding national infrastructure, especially oil pipelines, are strategic for the Niger Delta region and national economy. Tantita’s operations ensured the security of oil pipelines, uninterrupted flow of

item.title: Mitch McConnell releases health update, says he is ‘regaining’ strength
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Republican senator has been absent from Congress during a weeks-long hospitalisation, spurring party concern.

item.title: Gold Declines as Renewed US-Iran Strikes Raise Rate-Hike Bets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Gold declined after the US and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, sending energy prices higher and once more raising the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

item.title: Landslide kills 4 children, 1 adult in Cross River
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A rainfall-induced landslide has claimed the lives of four siblings and one adult in Ikot Awatim Community, Ikot Ansa, Calabar Municipality, the Cross River State Emergency Management Agency (SEMA) has confirmed. The post Landslide kills 4 children, 1 adult in Cross River appeared first on The Sun N

item.title: Oloja chieftaincy tussle: Court stops Oba, AG, others, directs parties to maintain status quo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Justice Olalekan A. Oresanya of the Lagos State High Court has ordered all parties in the dispute over the Oloja of Lagos chieftaincy stool to maintain the status quo pending the determination of the substantive suit. The post Oloja chieftaincy tussle: Court stops Oba, AG, others, directs parties to

item.title: Bandit kingpin Kachalla Yellow killed in Zamfara, Borno youths rally against terrorism
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Hunters and operatives of the Civilian Joint Task Force (CJTF) have neutralised a notorious bandit kingpin, Kachalla Sani Yellow, during a gun battle in Anka Local Government, Zamfara State. The post Bandit kingpin Kachalla Yellow killed in Zamfara, Borno youths rally against terrorism appeared firs

item.title: Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of strikes between U.S. and Iran
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices rose and U.S. stock-index futures slipped on Sunday, after the U.S. and Iran continued their tit-for-tat attacks around the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend.

item.title: US launches new wave of strikes against Iran, aimed at ‘degrading’ military
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Sunday's attacks follow three previous days of tit-for-tat strikes between Iran and US as a June ceasefire deal frays.

item.title: LSSTF-CACCOV to hold cybersecurity campaign against crime
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Lagos State Security Trust Fund’s Campaign Against Crime, Cultism and Other Vices (LSSTF-CACCOV) has warned that cybercrime continues to pose a serious threat, resulting in devastating financial losses, phishing attacks, cyberbullying, misinformation, identity theft and other forms of online cri

item.title: Ratajski beats Wattimena to lift European Darts Open title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Krzysztof Ratajski defeated Jermaine Wattimena 8-6 in a nail-biting final to win the European Darts Open in Leverkusen.

item.title: Woman uses 3-year-old son as cover to smuggle heroin into Abuja — NDLEA
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has arrested a 38-year-old South African woman, Will Jessica Ann, for allegedly using her three-year-old son as cover in an attempt to smuggle 5.75 kilograms of heroin into the country through the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja. The post

item.title: GOC warns troops against harassment, extortion, misconduct
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The General Officer Commanding (GOC), 81 Division Nigerian Army, Major General Adebayo Babalola, has warned troops deployed at the Forward Operating Base (FOB), Imeko, Ogun State, against harassment, extortion and all forms of misconduct, stressing that indiscipline and actions capable of tarnishing

item.title: World Cup 2026: The song everybody is singing but leaves the artiste in chains
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The song itself is an irony. While it has enabled millions to free themselves from desire and enter a state of euphoric Buddhist Nirvana across the globe, it has left the singer in tatters. Gala may be tired, as she always says, but she has not given up and is still fighting to recover the rights to

item.title: Papers: Arsenal turn to Alvarez with Barcola unavailable
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Monday's newspapers...

item.title: Nigeria’s oil output hits 74-month high, beats OPEC quota
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The report said the improved performance was primarily driven by stable production operations across most producing assets and the absence of any major pipeline outages during the period under review.

item.title: APC Beats INEC Deadline, Says Opposition Unprepared For 2027
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) said it successfully met the Independent National Electoral Commission’s (INEC) deadline for the submission of its candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections, while accusing opposition parties of exposing their lack of preparedness

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: By Tunde Rahman When Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew walked into Phase III Section of the Federal Secretariat in Abuja with a forged letter and emerged with an office, signage, and a semblance of government legitimacy, he did not act alone. That is the uncomfortable truth of the “Presidential Foreign

item.title: Middle East live: US strikes Iran as Gulf states targeted in flareup over Hormuz
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US military on Sunday said it had launched a new wave of attacks against Iran, after renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz saw several of Washington's Gulf allies targeted by incoming fire. The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at e

item.title: NIMC Registers 137m Nigerians As Tinubu Orders Full NIN Enrolment
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) has enrolled more than 137 million Nigerians into the National Identification Number (NIN) database as President Bola Tinubu directed the commission to ensure every Nigerian is registered before the end of 2026. The Director-General and Chief Execut

item.title: 2027: Tinubu-Shettima And The Politics Of Loyalty, Capacity, Continuity
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: By Stanley Nkwocha In politics, the most powerful decisions are sometimes the ones that end speculation. President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s decision to retain Vice President Kashim Shettima as his running mate for the 2027 presidential election is one of such decisions. It is a statement of trust, a sign

item.title: Oyo abduction: Islamic cleric hails Tinubu, Makinde over rescue of students, teachers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: An Islamic cleric and the first Muqaddamul Wai’zeen of Oyo State, Fadeelatus-Sheikh Abdul-Mu’min Shehu Usman, has applauded President Bola Ahmed, Governor Seyi Makinde, Soun of Ogbomoso, Oba Afolabi Olaoye, security agencies and other stakeholders who facilitated the rescue of the students and teach

item.title: Massive Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people, Thai officials say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 27 people were killed and 63 injured after a fire tore through a Bangkok bar late on Sunday, Thai officials said. Bangkok Governor Chadchart Sittipunt said the blaze spread rapidly, with smoke believed to be the main cause of death. Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause.

item.title: Bellingham and Tuchel's ‘battle of wills’ is healthy for England
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Passionate, driven, uncompromising. Those are terms that can be equally applied to both Thomas Tuchel and Jude Bellingham.

item.title: ADC Chairman David Mark Urges Graduates To Embrace Integrity, Innovation, Lifelong Learning
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Senator David Mark, has urged graduating students of Pace Setters College, Abuja, to embrace integrity, hard work and lifelong learning as they prepare to face the challenges of a rapidly changing world. Speaking on Sunday as the guest

item.title: Alavin Withdraws Claims Against Wizkid’s Associate Godson
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Emmanuel Jakpa, popularly known as Alavin, has withdrawn allegations contained in a viral video against businessman and social media influencer Uyi Godson Agho, popularly known as Godson, urging members of the public to disregard the recording. The retraction comes days after Godson, an associate of

item.title: Shettima’s Retention Signals APC’s Strength In North, Youth Parliament Declares

item.description: The APC Youth Parliament has expressed confidence that President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s decision to retain vice president Kashim Shettima as his running mate for the 2027 general elections will strengthen the ruling party’s chances of victory, particularly in the North-East and the Northern

item.description: Abuja, Nigeria – Nestlé Nigeria and Activate Success International Foundation (ASIF) reaffirm their existing eight-year partnership to advance youth entrepreneurship and empowerment across the country. Building on a robust history

item.title: MTN Nigeria Unveils Bundled Broadband, Router Plans to Boost Internet Access
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: MTN Nigeria has introduced bundled broadband solutions that combine data plans with compatible routers as part of efforts to expand broadband penetration and improve internet access for homes, small businesses

item.title: Show HN: Codebase Posters – turn any Git repo into generative poster art
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description:
npx codebase-posters inside any git repo opens a local exhibition: 18 pieces painted live from your commit history.everything renders from git log, nothing leaves your machine.
Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48885500 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: Man beats Wife to Death in Nasarawa
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: By Abubakar Abdullahi, Lafia A 30-year-old man, Musbawu Amodu, has beaten his 20-year-old wife to death in Angwan Yahaya, Agyaragu, Obi Local Government Area of Nasarawa State. The man, who has been arrested by the police, claimed he acted in anger after his wife refused to prepare food. According t

item.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

item.title: Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Uruguay has appointed legendary former striker Diego Forlan as interim head coach of the national team following Marcelo Bielsa's resignation after La Celeste's disappointing group-stage exit at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach appeared first on Vanguard N

item.title: Oil Climbs, US Stock Futures Dip on Iran Strikes: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil rose and US equity-index futures fell after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran, while conflicting claims over the status of the Strait of Hormuz heightened uncertainty.

item.title: Abbas hails Shettima’s APC re-nomination
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: He also commended President Tinubu for retaining confidence in Shettima, saying the decision reflected strong leadership and reinforced unity within the ruling APC. The post Abbas hails Shettima’s APC re-nomination appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: A spokesman for the 71-year-old South Carolina senator says initial findings suggest Graham's cause of death was related to his heart.

item.title: Oil Jumps as US and Iran Trade Strikes, Dispute Hormuz Status
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil jumped as the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, with the sides offering conflicting statements on whether the Strait of Hormuz remains open to shipping.

item.title: Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: She stressed that the National Identification Number (NIN) remains a unique identifier, ensuring that every individual is registered only once. The post Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: MeCure Industries seeks N50bn capital raise as profit jumps 177%
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: MeCure Industries Plc has proposed increasing its capital-raising programme to N50 billion from N30 billion as the pharmaceutical manufacturer accelerates its expansion across Nigeria and West Africa. The proposal followed the company’s impressive 2025 financial performance, with revenue risin

item.title: FG arrests two suspects in Osun illegal mining crackdown
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government has intensified its nationwide crackdown against illegal mining with the arrest of two suspected illegal miners during a coordinated enforcement operation at an illegal mining site in Osun State. The Special Assistant on Media to the Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Lar

item.title: Detained by settlers, US Democrat Ro Khanna now faces pro-Israel attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Progressive congressman accuses Israeli military of 'lying' after settlers held up his van in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian k

item.description: Arsenal want to sign Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, Aston Villa hijack Newcastle's move for Johan Manzambi, plus more.

item.description: Nigeria’s two largest listed telecommunications companies, MTN Nigeria Plc and Airtel Africa Plc, added a combined N5.67tn to their market capitalisation within the first 10 trading days of the second half of 2026, defying a broad sell-off on the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) that erased gai

item.description: The former defence spokesman's comment came days after the presidency and military authorities announced the rescue of the abductees. The post Ex-Defence Spokesman Enenche Hails Rescue Of Oyo Abductees appeared first on Channels Television.

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Achieving the Federal Government’s vision of growing Nigeria into a $1tn economy by 2030 will not be a walk in the park. It requires a stable and prosperous economy driven by peace and security. The peace and stability of the Niger Delta, coupled with the protection of critical national assets

item.title: Israel releases video of huge detonations in Lebanon
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Israeli military has released video showing huge explosions and detonations in villages in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Ukraine PM exits as Zelensky effects wartime cabinet change
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Prime Minister of Ukraine, Y​uliia Svyrydenko, has stepped down from the position after one year in office, President Volodymyr Zelensky announced on Sunday. Svyrydenko​ became the 19th Prime Minister ​on July 17, 2025, after being approved by the ​parliament. She previously served as First Depu

item.title: Police arrest man for allegedly beating wife to death in Nasarawa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to the suspect's confession, he acted out of anger after the deceased allegedly refused to prepare food, following an argument that ensued, which escalated into a physical altercation that ultimately resulted in her death; however, he expressed remorse over his actions. The post Police arr

item.title: Transfer: Trossard agrees deal to leave Arsenal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Arsenal forward, Leandro Trossard, is close to agreeing personal terms with Besiktas ahead of a summer move. According to The Athletic, the agreement between both clubs is in it final stages. Besiktas will pay the Premier League champions a fee around €18m upfront and €2m in add-ons. Trossard is exp

item.title: Transfer: Portuguese club SC Braga sign Nigerian youngster
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Portuguese Primeira Liga club SC Braga have completed the signing of Nigerian defender Adebisi Olamikun from Inter Lagos, DAILY POST reports. The Minho Warriors beat competition from other top clubs in Europe to the signature of the young centre-back. Olamikun signed a long-term contract with SC Bra

item.title: Lindsey Graham likely died from aortic rupture, medical examiner says
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Donald Trump and a leading Republican voice on foreign policy, died on Saturday after a brief illness, his office said. He was 71. The District of Columbia medical examiner said the preliminary cause of death was an aortic dissection linked to art

The army said that the troops rescued four individuals who had been kidnappe

item.description: Netanyahu, Israel's longest-serving prime minister, has confirmed that he will run for office again.

item.title: Argentina Legend Antonio Rattín Dies At 89 Ahead Of England World Cup Showdown
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Argentina football legend Antonio Rattín, the former national team captain famously sent off during the 1966 FIFA World Cup quarter-final against England, has died at the age of 89. Rattín’s death was confirmed by his lifelong club Boca Juniors on Saturday, coinciding with Argentina and England both

item.title: Sinner shatters Zverev’s Wimbledon dream, retains crown

item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner fought back from a set down to defeat Alexander Zverev and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday.

item.title: US Says It Began Launching More Strikes Against Iran at 5pm ET
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US Central Command forces began launching more strikes against Iran at 5 p.m. ET today, to continue degrading their ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships freely transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to a post on X.

item.description: Fourteen suspected hard drug dealers have been arrested by the Yobe State Police Command. He stated that the development was in continuation of sustained operations against criminal activiti

The defender left Bayelsa United following the club’s relegation from the NPFL. Williams featured regularly for the P

item.title: FG cracks down on illegal mining, arrests two suspects in Osun
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Government, through this operation, has reiterated that there will be no safe haven for illegal miners or their sponsors, as efforts to sanitise the sector and reposition it as a major driver of national economic growth remain irreversible. The post FG cracks down on illegal mining, arr

item.title: Iran expands attacks on Gulf states after intense US strikes
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iran reported strikes Sunday evening on two of its southern islands while Kuwait, where Tehran has repeatedly targeted US installations, said border posts and an offshore oil platform had been attacked. The as-yet unclaimed strikes came hours after Tehran and WashingtonStrait of Hormuz, offering con

item.title: The State of MCP Security [pdf]
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.canopii.dev/State%20of%20MCP%20Security%202026.pdf Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48884647 Points: 16 # Comments: 2

item.title: Fire engulfs popular Bangkok pub, killing 27 people and injuring 63
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Footage shared online by first responders showed a huge blaze raging in the Na Ladprao pub.

item.title: FG Arrests Two Supected Illegal Miners, Shuts Illegal Mining Site In Osun
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has arrested two suspected illegal miners and shut down an illegal mining site in Osun State during a coordinated enforcement operation targeting illicit mining activities. According to a report by The Punch, the suspects, Danladi Isa and Musa Kabiru, both in their twenties an

item.title: 2027: Northern group endorses Makinde for president
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A political support group, the Northern Nigeria Support Group for Seyi Makinde, has organised a special prayer session and a horse race in Kano to canvass support for the Oyo State Governor ahead of the 2027 presidential election. Speaking during the event, the National President of the group, Baban

item.title: Atiku Faults Proposed ₦50,000 Uniform WAEC, NECO Fees
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Vice President and African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has criticised the federal government over the reported increase in fees for Federal Unity Colleges and the proposed uniform ₦50,000 examination fee for candidates sitting the West African Senior Scho

item.title: Nigeria’s daily oil production rises by 2.3% to 1.74mbpd
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Data released by the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) on Sunday showed that average crude oil production, excluding condensates, increased to 1.56 million barrels per day in June from 1.53 million barrels per day in May. The post Nigeria’s daily oil production rises by

item.title: Tinubu’s Re-Nomination Of Shettima Shows Stability – Plang
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Senator Diket Plang, representing Plateau Central Senatorial District, has described President Bola Tinubu’s decision to retain Vice President Kashim Shettima on the All Progressives Congress, APC, 2027 presidential ticket as a demonstration of stability, courage and continuity in governance.

item.title: Gianni Infantino backs 64-team World Cup expansion plans
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has backed discussions over expanding the FIFA World Cup to 64 teams, noting that football’s biggest read more Gianni Infantino backs 64-team World Cup expansion plans
